message:
feat(robot): add pagination hints and serve improvements

Enhance robot API outputs with pagination helper fields in _agent_hints
to simplify client-side pagination handling.

Pagination improvements:
- Add NextOffset and PagesRemaining to HistoryAgentHints
- Add paginationHintOffsets() helper to compute next offset and pages
- Include _agent_hints with pagination info in StatusOutput
- Include _agent_hints with pagination info in SnapshotOutput
- Add unit tests for pagination hint offset calculation

Serve improvements (internal/cli/serve.go):
- Add --public-base-url flag for external client configuration
- Add structured logging with slog for server startup
- Include new PublicBaseURL in serve.Config

Server updates (internal/serve/server.go, server_test.go):
- Support PublicBaseURL configuration
- Related test updates

New E2E test:
- tests/e2e/handoff_cli_test.go: Tests for handoff create/list/show CLI

README.md: Documentation updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1kvaf","title":"CLI: Add --scan-dir flag for project directory","description":"## Purpose
Implement --scan-dir flag for ntm swarm command to specify the directory to scan for projects with open beads.

## Implementation Details

```go
// In cmd/ntm/swarm.go

var swarmCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "swarm",
    Short: "Orchestrate weighted multi-project agent swarm",
    Long:  `Scan projects for open beads and allocate agents proportionally.`,
    RunE:  runSwarm,
}

var (
    scanDir       string
    dryRun        bool
    configPath    string
)

func init() {
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&scanDir, "scan-dir", "", 
        "Directory to scan for projects (required)")
    swarmCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&dryRun, "dry-run", false,
        "Show allocation plan without executing")
    swarmCmd.Flags().StringVar(&configPath, "config", "",
        "Path to swarm config file (default: ~/.config/ntm/swarm.yaml)")
    
    // scan-dir is required
    swarmCmd.MarkFlagRequired("scan-dir")
}

func runSwarm(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    logger := slog.Default()
    
    // Validate scan-dir exists and is a directory
    info, err := os.Stat(scanDir)
    if err != nil {
        logger.Error("scan-dir validation failed",
            "path", scanDir,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("--scan-dir %q: %w", scanDir, err)
    }
    if !info.IsDir() {
        return fmt.Errorf("--scan-dir %q is not a directory", scanDir)
    }
    
    // Expand to absolute path
    absPath, err := filepath.Abs(scanDir)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("resolve absolute path: %w", err)
    }
    scanDir = absPath
    
    logger.Info("scan directory configured",
        "path", scanDir,
        "dry_run", dryRun)
    
    // Continue with scanner...
    return nil
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log the configured scan directory path
- Log whether path was relative (and resolved absolute)
- Include in all subsequent operation logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flag is marked as required
- [ ] Directory existence validation
- [ ] Is-directory validation (not file)
- [ ] Absolute path resolution
- [ ] Clear error messages on validation failure
- [ ] Value accessible throughout swarm operation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:02:02.903790931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T16:50:44.315427884Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T15:59:19.237959915Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1kvaf","depends_on_id":"bd-1ptvw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1kvaf","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1l4z","title":"Unit Tests: YAML swarm config parsing and validation","description":"## Test Coverage
Test YAML swarm configuration parsing.

## Test Cases

### Valid Config Parsing
- Parse complete swarm.yaml
- Parse minimal config (session + agents only)
- Parse config with all optional fields
- Numeric values parsed correctly
- Boolean values parsed correctly

### Default Values
- Missing fields get sensible defaults
- stuck_threshold defaults to 300
- auto_restart defaults to false

### Validation
- Require session name
- Require at least one agent type
- Reject negative agent counts
- Reject invalid agent types
- Reject malformed YAML

### Prompts Section
- Multi-line prompts preserved
- Template variables allowed
- Empty prompts allowed

### Error Messages
- Clear error for missing required field
- Line number in error for syntax issues
- Helpful suggestions for typos

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-1lca","title":"Task: Implement buildRecoveryContext in spawn.go","description":"## Purpose
Create the core function that assembles recovery context from all available sources.

## Function Signature
func (s *SpawnCmd) buildRecoveryContext(sessionName string) (*RecoveryContext, error)

## Context Sources
1. Latest checkpoint (if exists)
2. Agent Mail inbox (recent messages)
3. CM procedural memories
4. BV in-progress beads

## RecoveryContext Struct
type RecoveryContext struct {
    Checkpoint      *Checkpoint
    LastWorkingDir  string
    GitState        GitState
    RecentMessages  []AgentMailMessage
    Memories        []MemoryRule
    ActiveBeads     []Bead
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Function collects context from all available sources
- Graceful handling when sources unavailable
- Context includes timestamps for staleness detection
- Returns nil when no recovery context available

## Technical Notes
Use goroutines with WaitGroup for parallel queries
Set timeouts to avoid blocking spawn","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:12.439218243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:28:25.819788456Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:28:25.819718615Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: buildRecoveryContext() at spawn.go:1225 integrates all 4 context sources (checkpoint, Agent Mail, BV, CM) with graceful degradation, timestamps, and token limiting","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lca","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1m6sw","title":"DCG: Implement dcg binary detection and availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect if dcg binary is available on PATH.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'dcg' binary exists on PATH using exec.LookPath
2. Verify dcg version is compatible (parse 'dcg --version' output)
3. Cache result to avoid repeated lookups
4. Graceful degradation if dcg not found (log warning, continue without)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/dcg/detect.go

```go
package dcg

import (
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
)

var (
    once     sync.Once
    available bool
    version  string
)

func IsAvailable() bool {
    once.Do(detect)
    return available
}

func detect() {
    path, err := exec.LookPath("dcg")
    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    // Run 'dcg --version' and parse
    // Set available = true if compatible
}
```

## Testing
- Test with dcg installed
- Test without dcg installed
- Test with incompatible dcg version","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:44:08.896719039Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:48:38.239365538Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:48:38.239315283Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1m6sw","depends_on_id":"bd-2q9px","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1mcvs","title":"Task: Add rate_limit_detected field with pattern matching","description":"## What

Add `rate_limit_detected` boolean field that pattern-matches pane output
for rate limit indicators.

## Why

Rate limiting is the #1 failure mode in multi-agent sessions. Operators
currently must manually grep pane output to detect this. Automated detection
enables automated response (wait, switch account, skip pane).

## Rate Limit Patterns

Known patterns to detect:

| Provider | Pattern |
|----------|---------|
| Claude/Anthropic | "You've hit your limit" |
| Claude/Anthropic | "resets .* (America/.*)" |
| OpenAI | "Rate limit exceeded" |
| OpenAI | "Too many requests" |
| Google | "RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED" |
| Generic | "rate limit" (case insensitive) |

## Implementation

```go
var rateLimitPatterns = []*regexp.Regexp{
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)you've hit your limit`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)rate limit`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`(?i)too many requests`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED`),
    regexp.MustCompile(`resets \d+[ap]m`),
}

func detectRateLimit(paneContent string) (bool, string) {
    // Check last 50 lines only (performance)
    lines := strings.Split(paneContent, "\n")
    if len(lines) > 50 {
        lines = lines[len(lines)-50:]
    }
    content := strings.Join(lines, "\n")
    
    for _, pattern := range rateLimitPatterns {
        if match := pattern.FindString(content); match != "" {
            return true, match
        }
    }
    return false, ""
}
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "panes": [{
    "index": 4,
    "rate_limit_detected": true,
    "rate_limit_match": "You've hit your limit",
    "rate_limit_reset": "4pm ET"  // Parsed if available
  }]
}
```

## Performance

- Only check last 50 lines (configurable)
- Compile patterns once at startup
- Short-circuit on first match

## Configuration

Allow custom patterns via config for unknown providers:
```yaml
rate_limit_patterns:
  - "custom rate limit message"
```

## Testing

- Unit test: Each pattern with mock content
- Integration test: Actual rate-limited pane","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:18:44.314512352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:56:06.806832590Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:56:06.806782305Z","close_reason":"Task already implemented. Rate limit detection is fully functional:

1. rateLimitPatterns defined in internal/robot/status_enrich.go:28-34 with:
   - (?i)you've hit your limit
   - (?i)rate limit
   - (?i)too many requests
   - RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
   - resets \d+[ap]m

2. detectRateLimit() function at lines 154-161
3. enrichAgentStatus() integrates detection at lines 74-77
4. Agent struct has RateLimitDetected and RateLimitMatch fields
5. All rate limit tests pass (TestBackoffManager_RecordRateLimit, TestParseRateLimitWait, etc.)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1mcvs","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-22nh8","title":"EPIC: NTM Cockpit Simplification + Flywheel Tool Integration (Robot-first)","description":"## Goal
Make NTM the single, low-confusion cockpit for multi-agent work by (1) collapsing duplicate orchestration entry points, and (2) integrating the remaining Flywheel tools via robot-first interfaces.

## Background
Recent ideation created overlapping commands/configs (assign/swarm/distribute/YAML/workflow tiers). This risks user confusion and maintenance drag. At the same time, NTM should be the nerve center for Flywheel tooling, especially for agents using `--robot-*` modes.

## Scope
1. **Canonical orchestration**: one assignment flow (`ntm assign` with `--strategy`), one convenience entry (`ntm spawn --assign`).
2. **Orchestration model consolidation**: reuse existing pipeline/recipe system; no new workflow engine.
3. **Discovery simplification**: replace tiered gating with `help --minimal/--full` and optional suggestions.
4. **Prompt history UX**: UI/replay only; no new storage layer.
5. **Flywheel integrations**: add missing tool bridges (DCG, SLB, RU, MS, ACFS, GIIL, XF, UBS, JFP) with consistent robot outputs and tool health reporting.

## Non-Goals
- No new YAML swarm config or workflow DSL.
- No tiered command gating or promotion tracking.
- No new prompt-history storage subsystem.

## Success Criteria
- One clearly documented assignment flow and no duplicate commands.
- Pipelines/recipes are the sole orchestration template system.
- Help output is concise by default but fully discoverable.
- Prompt history UX is fully usable without new storage.
- All missing Flywheel tools are detectable and usable in robot mode.","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-22ns","title":"Task: Define effectiveness metrics","description":"## Purpose
Establish quantitative metrics for measuring agent session effectiveness.

## Scope
- Define metrics: completion rate, retry count, time to completion, token efficiency
- Create EffectivenessScore struct in internal/scoring/metrics.go
- Support custom metrics via configuration

## Acceptance Criteria
- Core metrics defined and documented
- Metrics are objective and measurable
- Score normalization for comparison
- Weights configurable per use case

## Technical Notes
- Consider task complexity as normalizing factor
- Avoid metrics that incentivize gaming","notes":"Implemented comprehensive effectiveness metrics in internal/scoring/metrics.go:

**Core Metrics (7 total)**:
- Completion: Task completion rate (tasks_completed / tasks_assigned)
- Retries: First-attempt success rate (1 - retries/max_retries)  
- TimeEfficiency: Speed vs estimate (min(1, estimated/actual))
- TokenEfficiency: Token economy (min(1, baseline/actual))
- Quality: Composite of tests_passing + no_regressions
- ErrorRate: Error avoidance (1 - errors/total_ops)
- ContextUsage: Context window efficiency (1 - avg_usage)

**Weight Configurations**:
- DefaultWeights: Completion 30%, Quality 25%, TokenEfficiency 15%, TimeEfficiency 10%, Retries 10%, ErrorRate 5%, ContextUsage 5%
- SpeedFocusedWeights: TimeEfficiency 30%
- QualityFocusedWeights: Quality 35%, ErrorRate 10%
- EconomyFocusedWeights: TokenEfficiency 25%, ContextUsage 10%

**Features**:
- RawMetrics.ToEffectivenessScore() for converting measurements to normalized scores
- WeightsConfig for file-based configuration with task-type overrides
- LoadWeightsConfig() for loading from JSON
- EffectivenessScore.WithWeights() for recomputing with different weights
- Comprehensive edge case handling (zero values, capping at 1.0)

All 10 tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:01:28.921882110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:28.627765251Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:28.627678077Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22ns","depends_on_id":"bd-1unv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22vun","title":"E2E Tests: ntm errors command with real tmux sessions","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end tests for the ntm errors command with real tmux sessions and error injection.

## Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_ntm_errors.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-ERRORS]"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_errors_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up test session: $TEST_SESSION"
    tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting ntm errors E2E tests"

# Setup: Create test session
log "Setup: Creating test session with multiple panes"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -v
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -h
sleep 0.5

# Inject errors into different panes
log "Injecting test errors into panes"

# Pane 0: Python error
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.0" 'echo "Traceback (most recent call last):" && echo "  File \"test.py\", line 1" && echo "FileNotFoundError: test.txt"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Pane 1: Go panic
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.1" 'echo "panic: runtime error: index out of range [5]" && echo "goroutine 1 [running]:"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Pane 2: Normal output
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.2" 'echo "INFO: Everything is fine"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Test 1: Capture all errors
log "Test 1: Capture all errors"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="$TEST_SESSION" 2>&1 || echo '{"errors":[]}')
log "Result: $RESULT"

ERROR_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.total_errors // .errors | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "Error count: $ERROR_COUNT"

if [[ "$ERROR_COUNT" -ge "2" ]]; then
    pass "Found errors in session"
else
    fail "Expected at least 2 errors, got $ERROR_COUNT"
fi

# Test 2: Filter by pane
log "Test 2: Filter by specific pane"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="$TEST_SESSION" --panes=0 2>&1 || echo '{"errors":[]}')
log "Result for pane 0: $RESULT"

HAS_PYTHON_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.errors[] | select(.line | contains("Traceback"))' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [[ -n "$HAS_PYTHON_ERROR" ]]; then
    pass "Found Python error in pane 0"
else
    fail "Missing Python error in pane 0"
fi

# Test 3: Check error structure
log "Test 3: Verify error structure"
FIRST_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.errors[0]' 2>/dev/null || echo "{}")
log "First error structure: $FIRST_ERROR"

HAS_PANE=$(echo "$FIRST_ERROR" | jq 'has("pane")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
HAS_LINE=$(echo "$FIRST_ERROR" | jq 'has("line")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")

if [[ "$HAS_PANE" == "true" ]] && [[ "$HAS_LINE" == "true" ]]; then
    pass "Error structure is correct"
else
    fail "Error structure missing fields"
fi

# Test 4: Non-existent session
log "Test 4: Non-existent session error handling"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="nonexistent_session_xyz" 2>&1 || echo '{"error":""}')
log "Result: $RESULT"

HAS_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq 'has("error")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
if [[ "$HAS_ERROR" == "true" ]]; then
    pass "Proper error for non-existent session"
else
    fail "Missing error for non-existent session"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E ntm errors Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

[[ $FAILED -eq 0 ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test error capture from multiple panes
- [ ] Test pane filtering (--panes flag)
- [ ] Test error structure (pane, timestamp, line fields)
- [ ] Test non-existent session error handling
- [ ] Test Python traceback detection
- [ ] Test Go panic detection
- [ ] Proper cleanup on exit

 {"id":"bd-23ot","title":"Task: Implement conflict notification","description":"## Purpose
Notify users when file reservation conflicts are detected.

## Conflict Scenario
Agent cc_1 tries to edit file.go
But file.go is reserved by cod_1

## Notification Display
⚠️File Conflict Detected

myproject__cc_1 tried to edit: internal/cli/send.go
But it is reserved by: myproject__cod_2 (since 5 minutes ago)

Options:
  1. Wait for reservation to expire (25m remaining)
  2. Request handoff from myproject__cod_2
  3. Force-release (requires user confirmation)

[1] Wait  [2] Request  [3] Force  [Esc] Dismiss

## Integration Points
- Dashboard notification component
- Alert system for urgent conflicts
- Action handlers for each option

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conflict detected and displayed clearly
- Options presented to user
- Actions execute correctly
- Notification dismissible

## Technical Notes
Use existing notification patterns
Integrate with Agent Mail force_release_file_reservation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:38.073189767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:56:03.398967279Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:56:03.398150411Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23ot","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-23reb","title":"EPIC: WebSocket Streaming + Event Bus","description":"## Summary\nProvide a real‑time WebSocket layer for pane output, alerts, beads/mail/scanner updates, and other event streams with replay/resume support.\n\n## Scope\n- WebSocket hub, topic router, subscription protocol, event envelope.\n- tmux pipe‑pane streaming integration + fallback capture‑pane polling.\n- Event persistence with ring buffers + cursor resume.\n- Backpressure handling + per‑client throttling options.\n- Event adapters for system, mail, beads, scanner, pipelines, approvals, alerts.\n\n## Success Criteria\n- Clients can subscribe to topics and resume from cursors.\n- Pane output streams reliably with low latency.\n- Backpressure does not crash server; dropped output is reported.\n","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:48:27.018334109Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:00:34.976027944Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-1kq4p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-3oeov","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-3s2wn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-cs5x4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-23reb","depends_on_id":"bd-excs1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3rngh","title":"Implement AccountRotator for caam integration","description":"## AccountRotator: Core Interface for Account Switching

### Purpose
Implement the AccountRotator that integrates with caam (coding_agent_account_manager) to switch between accounts when rate limits are hit.

### External Dependency: caam CLI
caam is a separate Rust CLI tool that manages OAuth credentials for AI coding agents. It provides <100ms account switching. NTM calls it via subprocess.

### Implementation Details

**File:** `internal/swarm/account_rotator.go`

**Interface:**
```go
// AccountRotator manages account rotation via caam CLI.
type AccountRotator struct {
    // caamPath is the path to caam binary (default: "caam")
    caamPath string
    
    // rotationHistory tracks rotations per session
    rotationHistory []RotationRecord
    
    // mu protects history
    mu sync.Mutex
}

// AccountInfo describes a caam account.
type AccountInfo struct {
    Provider    string    `json:"provider"`    // anthropic, openai, google
    AccountName string    `json:"account_name"`
    IsActive    bool      `json:"is_active"`
    LastUsed    time.Time `json:"last_used,omitempty"`
}

// RotationRecord tracks an account rotation.
type RotationRecord struct {
    Provider        string    `json:"provider"`
    FromAccount     string    `json:"from_account"`
    ToAccount       string    `json:"to_account"`
    RotatedAt       time.Time `json:"rotated_at"`
    SessionPane     string    `json:"session_pane"`
    TriggeredBy     string    `json:"triggered_by"` // "limit_hit", "manual"
}
```

**Core Methods:**
```go
// GetCurrentAccount returns the active account for a provider.
func (r *AccountRotator) GetCurrentAccount(provider string) (*AccountInfo, error)

// ListAccounts returns all accounts for a provider.
func (r *AccountRotator) ListAccounts(provider string) ([]AccountInfo, error)

// SwitchAccount switches to the next available account.
func (r *AccountRotator) SwitchAccount(provider string) (*RotationRecord, error)

// SwitchToAccount switches to a specific account.
func (r *AccountRotator) SwitchToAccount(provider, accountName string) (*RotationRecord, error)

// GetRotationHistory returns recent rotation records.
func (r *AccountRotator) GetRotationHistory(limit int) []RotationRecord

// IsAvailable checks if caam CLI is installed and working.
func (r *AccountRotator) IsAvailable() bool
```

**caam CLI Integration:**
```bash
# Check if caam is available
which caam

# Get current account
caam status --provider claude --json
# Output: {"provider": "claude", "active_account": "personal", ...}

# List accounts
caam list --provider claude --json
# Output: [{"name": "personal", "active": true}, {"name": "work", "active": false}]

# Switch to next account
caam switch --provider claude --next
# Or switch to specific
caam switch --provider claude --account work
```

**Provider Mapping:**
```go
var agentToProvider = map[string]string{
    "cc":  "claude",
    "cod": "openai",
    "gmi": "google",
}
```

### Graceful Degradation
If caam is not installed, AccountRotator should:
1. Log a warning on first use
2. Return errors indicating caam unavailable
3. AutoRespawner should continue without rotation

### Logging Requirements
- Log account query: `[AccountRotator] get_current provider=%s account=%s`
- Log switch start: `[AccountRotator] switch_start provider=%s from=%s`
- Log switch complete: `[AccountRotator] switch_complete provider=%s to=%s duration=%v`
- Log caam unavailable: `[AccountRotator] caam_unavailable error=%v`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] caam CLI integration via subprocess
- [ ] Account listing and current account detection
- [ ] Account switching (next and specific)
- [ ] Rotation history tracking
- [ ] Graceful degradation when caam unavailable
- [ ] Provider name normalization (cc->claude, etc)
- [ ] Detailed structured logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:37.266632641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:45:15.959350199Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T03:45:15.956785788Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rngh","depends_on_id":"bd-3hvz5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rngh","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3s2f","title":"EPIC: Webhook System for External Integrations","description":"## Overview
Implement an event-driven webhook system that triggers HTTP callbacks on NTM events, enabling integration with Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, CI/CD systems, and custom automation.

## Problem Statement
NTM operates as a closed system. Users can't:
- Get notified in Slack when agents complete work
- Trigger CI pipelines when all agents finish
- Alert PagerDuty when errors occur
- Build custom dashboards from live data
- Integrate with existing DevOps tooling

## Key Features
1. Configurable webhook endpoints per event type
2. Built-in formatters for Slack, Discord, Teams
3. Retry logic with exponential backoff
4. Event filtering by session, agent type, severity
5. Payload templates with variable substitution

## Event Types
- agent.started: Agent session created
- agent.completed: Agent finished task
- agent.error: Agent encountered error
- agent.idle: Agent became idle
- session.created: New NTM session
- session.ended: Session terminated
- bead.assigned: Bead assigned to agent
- bead.completed: Bead marked done

## Configuration (ntm.yaml)
webhooks:
  - url: https://hooks.slack.com/services/xxx
    events: [agent.completed, agent.error]
    formatter: slack
    filter:
      session: myproject*
      severity: [warning, error, critical]
  - url: https://my-api.com/ntm-events
    events: [*]
    formatter: json

## Success Criteria
- Webhooks fire reliably on configured events
- Built-in formatters produce valid Slack/Discord messages
- Failures don't block NTM operation
- Easy to configure without code changes

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/webhook/manager.go (new - webhook orchestration)
- internal/webhook/formatters/ (new - Slack, Discord, JSON formatters)
- internal/config/webhooks.go (new - webhook config parsing)
- internal/events/emitter.go (event emission points)","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-fj9f","title":"Task: Implement contextual command suggestions","description":"## Overview
Provide intelligent, context-aware command suggestions based on current NTM state.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/cli/suggestions/contextual.go:
   - SuggestNextCommand(state State) Suggestion
   - State struct capturing current NTM status
   - Suggestion struct with command, reason, example

2. Context detection:
   - No sessions running -> suggest spawn
   - Sessions running, none busy -> suggest send
   - Sessions running, all busy -> suggest status or dashboard
   - Recent errors -> suggest tail or logs
   - Many sessions -> suggest dashboard
   - Beads available -> suggest assign or swarm

3. Suggestion display:
   - Show at end of command output (non-intrusive)
   - Format: 'Next: ntm send myproject --msg="Start task"'
   - Make dismissible via config (suggestions_enabled: false)

4. Integration points:
   - After ntm status: suggest send if agents idle
   - After ntm spawn: suggest send
   - After ntm send: suggest status or tail
   - After error: suggest relevant recovery command

## Implementation Notes
- Keep suggestions brief (one line)
- Don't show suggestion if user seems to know what they're doing
- Rate limit to avoid being annoying

## Acceptance Criteria
- Suggestions appear in appropriate contexts
- Suggestions are actionable and relevant
- Can be disabled via config
- Unit tests for each context scenario","notes":"Optional, non-intrusive suggestions. Do NOT depend on tiers or usage tracking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonSparrow","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:25:23.670754891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:47.047170376Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:04:47.046000212Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fj9f","depends_on_id":"bd-vcmnv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-fv2hy","title":"Task: Implement real-time synthesis streaming","description":"## Implementation
Add streaming synthesis without altering non-stream behavior:

1. Add `SynthesisChunk` + stream method in existing synthesis code (prefer extending `internal/ensemble/synthesis.go`; only add a new file if unavoidable):
   - `StreamSynthesize(ctx, inputs) (<-chan SynthesisChunk, <-chan error)`
   - Chunk fields: type, content, mode_id, index, timestamp

2. Emission rules:
   - Deterministic ordering (monotonic index)
   - Progress/status chunks at key milestones
   - Backpressure-safe channel usage

3. CLI:
   - `ntm ensemble synthesize --stream` (human-readable incremental output)
   - `--stream --json` emits JSONL events + final summary
   - Ctrl+C cancels, writes checkpoint, prints resume command

4. Checkpoint integration:
   - On cancel/error, write partial checkpoint (bd-3didt)
   - On resume, continue emitting chunks from last index

## Logging
- Log stream start/end, chunk_count, time_to_first_chunk, cancel reason

## Tests
- Unit: chunk ordering + deterministic emission
- Integration: stream + synthesis fixture
- E2E: CLI streaming output and resume path

## Dependencies
- Synthesis System (bd-2qwm8)
- Synthesis strategies (bd-2ztw9)
- Partial recovery (bd-3didt)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:42:12.555031671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T21:54:33.978185967Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2nnsg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fv2hy","depends_on_id":"bd-3didt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-fy2cm","title":"Implement --dry-run and explicit allocation","description":"Implement --dry-run and explicit allocation modes.

## --dry-run Mode

Show allocation plan without executing:
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "planned_assignments": [
    {
      "pane": 2,
      "would_assign": "bd-92e1",
      "reason": "highest_unblocks (9)"
    }
  ]
}
```

Useful for:
- Reviewing before executing
- Integration testing
- What-if analysis

## Explicit Allocation (--allocation)

Accept JSON mapping of pane to bead:
```bash
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --allocation='{"2":"bd-92e1","3":"bd-55pm"}'
```

Bypasses bv triage, uses exactly what user specifies.

## --skip-panes Flag

Exclude specific panes from allocation:
```bash
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv --skip-panes=4,10
```

Useful when some panes are:
- Already working on something
- Rate limited (leave them alone)
- Reserved for manual control","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:31:40.064506799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:19.677061450Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:19.677020303Z","close_reason":"Implemented --dry-run and --allocation flags for bulk assign","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fy2cm","depends_on_id":"bd-t9qp4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fzhuh","title":"Implement --fix auto-remediation","description":"Implement --fix flag for automatic remediation.

## Behavior

When --fix is provided:
1. Run normal diagnosis
2. For each auto_fixable recommendation, execute fix_command
3. Report results of each fix attempt

## What Gets Fixed

- crashed: Restart agent via --robot-restart-pane
- unresponsive: Kill and restart via --robot-restart-pane

## What Does NOT Get Fixed

- rate_limited: Requires human decision (wait vs caam)
- unknown: Not enough info to act safely

## Output Enhancement

Add fix_results to response:
```json
{
  "recommendations": [...],
  "fix_results": [
    {
      "pane": 15,
      "attempted": true,
      "success": true,
      "command": "ntm --robot-restart-pane myproject 15",
      "output": {...}
    }
  ]
}
```

## Safety

- Only fix auto_fixable=true items
- Run fixes sequentially (avoid overwhelming system)
- Report partial success if some fixes fail","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:27:46.095589296Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.652649613Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.652594890Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fzhuh","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-fzhuh","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:07Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-g3p0g","title":"Unit Tests: SwarmOrchestrator session and pane management","description":"## Purpose
Unit tests for SwarmOrchestrator covering session creation, pane management, and full swarm lifecycle.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/swarm_orchestrator_test.go
package swarm

func TestSwarmOrchestrator_CreateSessions(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{t: t}
    orchestrator := NewSwarmOrchestrator(mockTmux, nil, testLogger(t))

    plan := &SwarmPlan{
        Sessions: []SessionSpec{
            {Name: "cc_agents_1", AgentType: "cc", PaneCount: 3},
            {Name: "cod_agents_1", AgentType: "cod", PaneCount: 2},
        },
    }

    t.Log("[TEST] Creating sessions from plan")
    t.Logf("[TEST] Plan has %d sessions", len(plan.Sessions))

    err := orchestrator.CreateSessions(plan)
    require.NoError(t, err)

    t.Logf("[TEST] Sessions created: %v", mockTmux.createdSessions)
    assert.Len(t, mockTmux.createdSessions, 2)
    assert.Contains(t, mockTmux.createdSessions, "cc_agents_1")
    assert.Contains(t, mockTmux.createdSessions, "cod_agents_1")
}

func TestSwarmOrchestrator_SpawnAgents(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{t: t}
    mockLauncher := &MockAgentLauncher{t: t}
    orchestrator := NewSwarmOrchestrator(mockTmux, mockLauncher, testLogger(t))

    session := SessionSpec{
        Name:      "cc_agents_1",
        AgentType: "cc",
        Panes: []PaneSpec{
            {Index: 1, Project: "/home/user/proj1", AgentType: "cc"},
            {Index: 2, Project: "/home/user/proj2", AgentType: "cc"},
        },
    }

    t.Log("[TEST] Spawning agents for session")

    err := orchestrator.SpawnAgentsInSession(session)
    require.NoError(t, err)

    t.Logf("[TEST] Agents spawned: %d", len(mockLauncher.launchedAgents))
    assert.Len(t, mockLauncher.launchedAgents, 2)
}

func TestSwarmOrchestrator_GracefulShutdown(t *testing.T) {
    mockTmux := &MockTmuxClient{
        t: t,
        sessions: []string{"cc_agents_1", "cod_agents_1"},
    }
    orchestrator := NewSwarmOrchestrator(mockTmux, nil, testLogger(t))

    t.Log("[TEST] Testing graceful shutdown")

    ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
    defer cancel()

    err := orchestrator.Shutdown(ctx)
    require.NoError(t, err)

    t.Logf("[TEST] Sessions killed: %v", mockTmux.killedSessions)
    assert.Len(t, mockTmux.killedSessions, 2)
}

func TestSwarmOrchestrator_SessionNaming(t *testing.T) {
    tests := []struct {
        agentType string
        num       int
        expected  string
    }{
        {"cc", 1, "cc_agents_1"},
        {"cod", 2, "cod_agents_2"},
        {"gmi", 3, "gmi_agents_3"},
    }

    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.expected, func(t *testing.T) {
            result := generateSessionName(tt.agentType, tt.num)
            t.Logf("[TEST] %s + %d = %s", tt.agentType, tt.num, result)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.expected, result)
        })
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test session creation from SwarmPlan
- [ ] Test agent spawning in sessions
- [ ] Test graceful shutdown
- [ ] Test session naming convention
- [ ] Test pane count verification
- [ ] Mock TmuxClient and AgentLauncher
- [ ] Detailed test logging with [TEST] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:34:49.682930987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:02:41.150799785Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:02:41.150743700Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-g3p0g","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:08Z","created_by":"import"}]}

+### API/WS Server Deployment
+
+For split hosting (recommended), run the API/WS daemon on a long-lived host and
+serve the Web UI separately (e.g., Vercel for UI, Fly.io/Render/bare metal for API/WS).
+
+Local-only (safe default):
+
+```bash
+ntm serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 7337
+```
+
+Remote bind (requires auth) + CORS allowlist:
+
+```bash
+ntm serve \
+  --host 0.0.0.0 \
+  --port 7337 \
+  --auth-mode api_key \
+  --api-key $NTM_API_KEY \
+  --cors-allow-origin https://ui.example.com \
+  --public-base-url https://api.example.com
+```
+
+Notes:
+- Non-loopback binds require an auth mode.
+- `--cors-allow-origin` controls both CORS and WebSocket origin checks.
+- `--public-base-url` advertises the externally reachable URL for clients.
+
 ### Building with Docker
